title: Eschweilera longipes
intro: Eschweilera longipes, also known as the Brazilian cherry, is a tropical plant species that belongs to the family Lecythidaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Eschweilera longipes include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Eschweilera longipes is classified within the family Lecythidaceae, commonly known as the Brazil nut family. This family is native to the Americas and consists of over 30 genera and 200 species. Eschweilera longipes is one of the many species within the genus Eschweilera, which comprises around 80 species. The genus Eschweilera is further divided into 10 sections, each containing multiple species. The classification of Eschweilera longipes is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic analysis.
